issue_title: error: misaka renders the first lines to <pre> & <code> ?
issue_body:
Hi, I'm having a weird issue with Misaka when rendering the markdown in my templates. I'm calling the plugin using the built in filter and a db call like so: ``` {% filter markdown %} {{exhibition.wysiwig_exhibition_description}} {% endfilter %} ``` which generates this markup: ``` 
    # virgile's bug

David Adamo’s oeuvre incorporates sculpture, performance, installation, and video. In his sculptures, Adamo has often manipulated familiar manufactured objects: canes, baseball bats, tools and axes carved and hacked away in such a way that they become disconnected from their intended use, the inherent strength of each transformed into a fragile ghost of what we expect.

something in the middle

Adamo’s new work pushes these transformations in new directions, not only subverting an object’s intrinsic use but also playing with shifts in size and mate- rial. Squashed tomatoes are cast in bronze; rolled-up carpets are carved from trunks of cedar wood. The sculptures can take on impressive dimensions or become tiny, inconspicuous objects.

``` based on this raw md from my document: ``` "wysiwig_exhibition_description" : "# virgile's bug\r\n\r\nDavid Adamo’s oeuvre incorporates sculpture, performance, installation, and video. In his sculptures, Adamo has often manipulated familiar manufactured objects: canes, baseball bats, tools and axes carved and hacked away in such a way that they become disconnected from their intended use, the inherent strength of each transformed into a fragile ghost of what we expect.\r\n\r\n## something in the middle\r\n\r\n*Adamo’s new work pushes these transformations in new directions, not only subverting an object’s intrinsic use but also playing with shifts in size and mate- rial. Squashed tomatoes are cast in bronze; rolled-up carpets are carved from trunks of cedar wood. The sculptures can take on impressive dimensions or become tiny, inconspicuous objects.*\r\n\r\nThe objects Adamo continuously transforms into artworks are always familiar objects, like household radiators, erasers and even cookies or pieces of fruit. They are domestic objects, objects that remind the artist of home, of family, of his child.\r\n", ``` Am I doing something wrong? Why would Misaka render everything properly except the first tagged block? Cheers!

issue_title: Improper segmentation with proper names where middle initial is abbreviated
issue_body:
I've been trying out segtok and comparing it against the segmentations produced by coreNLP, spacy, and punkt on the English wikipedia.  

Segtok is working well in some cases where the others fail.  However, there is one chronic case where segtok always splits prematurely which is particularly noticeable given wikipedia's encyclopedic nature. In particular, any sentence with <Given name> <Middle initial>. <Surname> seems to always split on the period after the middle initial.

Example of segtok splits:
By 1968, Leger had returned to Canada's capital and was appointed as under-secretary of state, providing the administrative basis for Prime Minister Lester B.
Pearson's foreign policy, and the policies on bilingualism and multiculturalism developed by the Cabinet chaired by Pearson's successor, Pierre Trudeau.

This simple model is commonly known as the adjacency list model, and was introduced by Dr. Edgar F.
Codd after initial criticisms surfaced that the relational model could not model hierarchical data.

Seems like a bug but perhaps it is by design.

issue_title: Add possibility to cancel bandpass filter operations
issue_body:
Currently, when a bandpass Filter is triggered, you need to wait until it is finished. It would be better to perform the filter in __another process__, so filtering can be aborted with ``` QKeySequence::Cancel ``` (Escape Button).

Furthermore, the GUI will stay responsive during filtering that way.

issue_title: stall effects
issue_body:
track stalling in runs and act based on them. 
stalling occurs when the engineered features are no longer improving either 1) the ML model CV score or 2) the median fitness of the features themselves. 
if stalling occurs, there should be options to 
 - exit 
 - modify search to capture a different part of the search space. this could be achieved by increasing complexity of the features, increasing variation steps, or lowering selection presure.

issue_title: Importing ipdb in ipython startup files breaks tab completion.
issue_body:
I don't know exactly why yet, but importing ``ipdb`` in IPython startup files (i.e. ``~/.ipython./profile_default/startup/``) breaks tab completion.

The issue has to do with line 32 in ``ipdb/__main__.py`` which tests for the global symbol ``get_ipython``.  At this early state of startup, the global symbol is not defined, and then something in the ``except NameError:`` block breaks the completer.  (Somehow the completer that can actually do completions is replaced with another broken completer.  Probably due to the embedding.)

Is there a more robust way of doing this test that will also work in the startup files?

issue_title: Quantities using multi-dimensional ndarrays fail to render using IPython.lib.latextools.latex_to_png
issue_body:
Let's start with the problem code. If you're using the Spyder IPython console (and I'd guess the IPython Qt console, though I haven't tested this), output of certain Quantities will fail:

```
In [1]: Q_(np.zeros((2, 3)), 'V')
Out[1]: Failed to render latex: 
$[[ 0.  0.  0.]
^
Expected end of text (at char 0), (line:1, col:1) 
```

The IPython console detects `Quantity._repr_latex_()` and uses its output, which contains newlines from `format(some_multidimensional_array)`. Spyder's IPython console uses `IPython.lib.latextools.latex_to_png()` to render the latex (unlike the IPython notebook, which instead uses `latex_to_html()`). This ultimately uses matplotlib's TeX parser, which chokes on multi-line input.

The parsing method that the IPython notebook uses does not choke on the newlines, but it does seem to strip them out.

The simplest course of action that makes sense to me is to remove any newlines in `Quantity._repr_latex_()` before returning the result.

issue_title: Add kitten run --interactive
python providers should use value_types as an keyword argument
issue_body:
Some of the python providers use a slightly unusual function call signature like this:

```
fake.pydict(nb_elements=10, variable_nb_elements=True, *value_types)
fake.pylist(nb_elements=10, variable_nb_elements=True, *value_types)

```
This leads to some usage problems with factory_boy (https://github.com/FactoryBoy/factory_boy/issues/387).

IMHO it would be much more convenient to be able to call the python providers with `value_types` as a keyword argument like so:

```
fake.pydict(nb_elements=10, variable_nb_elements=True, value_types=['str'])
fake.pylist(nb_elements=10, variable_nb_elements=True, value_types=['str'])

```

I know this would introduce a braking change to the python provider api, but if this is acceptable I can try to provide a PR.

issue_title: Mapping ONT reads to very similar reference sequences
issue_body:
Hi, Dr. Li,
I used "-ax map-ont" to map nanopore reads (generated using PCR amplicons from the HLA-C locus) to a reference file containing sequences for all HLA-C alleles. Some of these alleles are really similar with only 1-base differences, for example HLA-C\*03:03 and HLA-C\*03:04 (see attached image), and the variant is located at the end of exon2. Although this particular dataset is supposed to be HLA\*03:04, for reason unclear to me, the reads are mapped to both HLA-C\*03:03 and HLA-C\*03:04, with a slight advantage for HLA-C\*03:03. In contrast, if I use BLASR for read mapping (default settings), there will be much less reads mapped to the references, but the majority of reads are mapped to the correct allele HLA-C\*03:04. Is there an explanation for this observation? 
I would love to make minimap2 work for my pipeline because I can get more reads mapped. But is there a trade-off between accuracy and number of reads mapped? Overall, for 28 out of 30 samples, the minmap2 worked very well except for the example mentioned above and anther sample involving HLA-A\*33:01 versus 33:03. Thank you so much for the great tool and your help! 
![screenshot from 2017-12-24 17-38-22](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10225753/34329741-6fcf8cd6-e8d1-11e7-9116-fe1e9d865008.png)

issue_title: target/file specific cflags heritage
issue_body:
Hi Stefano,
Would it be possible to make the .cflags.heritage file so that it will keep track of the flags on a per-source and target basis? It may already do this when using a `fobos` file, but when run without a `fobos` each time FoBiS.py is run to build different targets, it overwrites the cflags used for the old target with those used for the new target. That means, that if two targets have different cflags, building either of them will cause FoBiS.py to think that the other one needs to be rebuilt due to stale cflags.

One idea is you could make files like this:
`.cflags.heritage.<target_name>` where `<target_name>` is the name of the thing you’re building.

Another idea is to have one line per target in `.cflags.heritage` with some sort of key at the beginning of the line. Then FoBiS.py can look up the cflags associated with the target it is working on, if they need updating, then rebuild the target and insert the new flags after the key.  You could even just delete the whole line the stale key/flags combo is on if a rebuild is needed due to the flags, and insert the same key with updated flags at the beginning or end of the file.

These lines might look like:
```
libjsonfortran.a : “-O3 -Wextra -Wall -pedantic”
```
where the key is just the target name, followed by a space or some special separator character and then the cflags.

Basically, I think you need a dictionary structure to store cflags at least on a per target basis. It’s possible that you may want to compile different sources for a given target with different flags too, but I’m not sure if FoBiS.py currently supports this, and if it does, I don’t know how to do it.

issue_title: Set last-modified time support?
issue_body:
Hi Martin

First of all sorry if this should not go under issues, as it may be more a question or feature request than a bug/issue.

One of our users tried out `rsync -a localdir mntdir` against an OSX Finder-mounted wsgidav volume and it actually mostly works, except the file time stamps are not transferred. Thus repeating the rsync call does not skip the already transferred files as intended. I repeated the test with fusedav from Linux and got similar results. Calling a simple `touch` on a file in the mount also gets rejected with "Operation not supported".
As far as I can tell from my tracing this is due to wsgidav refusing PROPPATCH calls with the {DAV:}getlastmodified argument. The relevant doc string in setPropertyValue of dav_provider correctly states that any {DAV:} property modification requests result in a HTTP_FORBIDDEN reply and that resource providers may override the method to "update supported custom live properties".

I did a quick test and added such set {DAV:}getlastmodified support directly in (fs_)dav_handler and then `touch`, `rsync -t` and `rsync -a` all work as expected. However, I'm not really sure if it belongs there or if it is even compliant with the WebDAV spec:
http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4918#section-15.7

It would be quite cool with rsync support for our purpose, but maybe I'm overlooking some problems it might introduce? Would it make sense to properly add such general set modification time support and maybe only enable it with a conf setting? I think I can give it a shot and send a pull request if so.
Or is that something you consider in the custom live properties category and better left for a derived fs resource provider?

Cheers, Jonas

issue_labels: [{'id': 725837142, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3MjU4MzcxNDI=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/cannatag/ldap3/labels/Fixed%20In%20upcoming%20version', 'name': 'Fixed In upcoming version', 'color': '63e28e', 'default': False}, {'id': 725836254, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3MjU4MzYyNTQ=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/cannatag/ldap3/labels/Waiting%20answer%20from%20user', 'name': 'Waiting answer from user', 'color': 'd4c5f9', 'default': False}, {'id': 161142985, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwxNjExNDI5ODU=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/cannatag/ldap3/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'fc2929', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
We have an LDAP server that validates OTP. The essence of OTP is that the same token is only ever valid once. On the first bind, it approves the token, and then on the second bind, it fails since it has already been used. This breaks the authentication.

# Code flow

`do_auto_bind` will first call `.open` for your strategy.
https://github.com/cannatag/ldap3/blob/43234e26909cd3a07e1570934d65752a9b3726de/ldap3/core/connection.py#L331-L336

The problem is that if you use the BaseStrategy (or anything that extends BaseStrategy that doesn't change this method), it will also call `do_auto_bind` near the end of its `open`:
https://github.com/cannatag/ldap3/blob/43234e26909cd3a07e1570934d65752a9b3726de/ldap3/strategy/base.py#L151-L155

This causes two binds to be performed. I'm not sure on what kind of fix you want, but adding a simple check for `if self.bound: return` in `do_auto_bind` should be sufficient. You can also protect the `.bind` method if it has already been bound.

issue_title: About find_candidate_object_locations
issue_body:
Hi, I don't know if this is the right place for this question, but I was wondering how find_candidate_object_locations actually finds the candidates. I was reading the description of what this function ensures, but I couldn't quite understand it. The description had referenced a paper "Segmentation as Selective Search for Object Recognition by Koen E. A. van de Sande, et al.", but said the implementation deviated from the paper slightly and to refer to the code, which unfortunately I don't understand either.

So my question is, how exactly is this function finding the candidates or bounding boxes of a given image?

issue_title: How to uninstall dlib Python bindings?
issue_body:
I want to remove Python bindings for dlib. It uses setup.py to install it, so I can not remove it via pip. I want to ask how to remove these bindings?

My OS is Linux Mint 18.2. I use Anaconda, so I want to install dlib via conda instead of building from source. However, I installed it from source first so I want to remove these bindings now. What is the best way to remove Python bindings?

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: Dlib performance on low resolution faces and retraining dlib.
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm working with Dlib face-recognition for quite a long time on surveillance-camaeras videos. The faces in the videos i'm working with are much smaller than those in the public datasets like LFW, on which Dlib is benchmarked. This difference causes a significant degradation in performance on my data comparing to the 99% accuracy on LFW and i suspect it is, up to some point, due to Dlib being trained on high resoltuion images like those inLFW.

A possible solution could be to lower the resolution of  dlib's train data or to pass it through a domain adapter so that it will be more similar to surveillance footage data and then retrain dlib on it.

Have you thought about such face-recognition net version that will work with low resolution faces. I think this might be practical for any integration of dlib on programs that works on video, where faces are usually much smaller than 255x255

Since the data dlib was trained on is not public i can't do this adaptation my self and i ask you:
- What is the average face\image size in dlib's face train dataset. Does it contain low reolution image or 
  is it more similar to LFW
- I there a way to buy this dataset ?
- Do you think such a retrained model will perform better on unseen low resolution faces? 

I'm using:
- Dlib: 19.9
- Cuda: 8.0
- cudnn: 5.1
- cmake: 3.9
- compiler: Visual C++ 14.0
- GPU: GeForce GTX 1070
- System:Windows

Thanks in advance,
Ariel.

issue_title: Adding Shake-Shake support
issue_body:
Shake-Shake Residual network has been introduced recently, it claims to achieve 2.72% error on CIFAR-10:

> This method aims at helping computer vision practitioners faced with an overfit problem. The idea is to replace, in a 3-branch ResNet, the standard summation of residual branches by a stochastic affine combination. The largest tested model improves on the best single shot published result on CIFAR-10 by reaching 2.72% test error.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3162787/23487816/d0ac688a-fefd-11e6-89ed-84d5656dcd38.png)


It would be nice to see dlib supporting Shake-Shake since it would improve results greatly, also, it's not so difficult to implement. Shake Shake needs some simple modification in add_prev to use a stochastic affine combination instead of the current addition and some modification to use 2 residual branches instead of the only 1. 

There is an implementation in torch:
https://github.com/xgastaldi/shake-shake
Here is the reference paper:
https://openreview.net/forum?id=HkO-PCmYl&noteId=HkO-PCmYl

issue_title: requests logging level exposes credentials
issue_body:
Currently, when using lexicon, the requests library logs every connection and request to the providers api.  I'm currently only using namecheap, which sadly includes the ApiUser and ApiKey as GET vars.  So, every use of lexicon exposes these values.  This becomes a problem, particularly when using lexicon as a library, when automated scripts expose and log these values. 

I'm not familiar with other providers, so I don't know how much of an issue this is generally.  I hope I'm just missing a switch to turn down the logging, but I can't find it if so.  I can set this in providers/namecheap.py:

logging.getLogger('requests').setLevel(logging.ERROR)

to remove the messages, but I don't know if this is appropriate, or if it warrants a wider solution.  I can see why these messages could be useful in debugging (they are debug level) but in general I don't want or need to see them.  

I don't know if there is a reason why the default logger is set to debug, but another option would be to set this higher as well.  e.g.

logging.basicConfig(stream=sys.stdout, level=logging.ERROR, format='%(message)s')

but, lots of useful output is logged at debug level (like most of the output).  What do you think?

issue_title: Handle on scandir iterator on Windows
issue_body:
On Windows, scandir will keep a handle on the directory being scanned until the iterator is exhausted. This behavior can cause various problems if try to use some filesystem calls like chmod or remove on the directory while the handle is still being kept.

This should be at least documented. Alternatively, it might be interesting to provide a way prematurely end the scan without having to exhaust it and close the handle.

issue_title: Check that docstrings are triple-double-quotes
issue_body:
There are many guidelines proposed within the [PEP257](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0257/) document. One of the easiest ones to check is that docstrings should always use triple-double-quotes """.

I don't know if this is out of scope for this module (since it's names `autopep8`), but I think a lot of the guidelines proposed in PEP257 would be nice additions to this program.

Hello,

When I use the following python code as an administrator on windows, some usernames are empty:
```
>> run pyshell

2.7.11 (v2.7.11:6d1b6a68f775, Dec  5 2015, 20:32:19) [MSC v.1500 32 bit (Intel)]

$owners = @{}

Why I can't see usernames (e.g SYSTEM) of some processes with _psutil.process_iter()_ while I can with powershell for example?

Thank you in advance for your help,

issue_title: Allow to apply transform to path
issue_body:
As described in https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Attribute/transform

it's common for svg `<path>` element to have `transform=""` attribute

typicaly it looks something like this:

```svg
<path id="something" d="..."  transform="rotate(-10 50 100)
                translate(-36 45.5)
                skewX(40)
                scale(1 0.5)" />
```

Can you please add possibility to apply this transformation if supplied to svg.path during parsing stage? In fact only support for **rotate(), translate() and scale()** is needed in most cases. It should be pretty easy to do.

In ideal case svg.path would take two individual strings: d attribute and transform attribute.
It would be also perfect, if i can just supply whole `<path ... />` string and svg.path would extract the data from it, so i don't need to parse it by myself. Or even whole SVG file, which would in turn get exploded into multiple paths.

Thanks

and the corresponding DIE output by pyelftools:

    |DW_AT_type        :  AttributeValue(name='DW_AT_type', form='DW_FORM_ref_addr', value=1093104, raw_value=1093104, offset=1093095)
    |10496             :  AttributeValue(name=10496, form='DW_FORM_data1', value=0, raw_value=0, offset=1093099)

    1093100 (0x10ADEC)
    DIE None, size=1, has_chidren=None

    1093101 (0x10ADED)
    DIE None, size=1, has_chidren=None

    1093102 (0x10ADEE)
    DIE None, size=1, has_chidren=None

    1093103 (0x10ADEF)
    DIE DW_TAG_typedef, size=22, has_chidren=False
    |DW_AT_name        :  AttributeValue(name='DW_AT_name', form='DW_FORM_string', value='\x15runtime.typeAlg', raw_value='\x15runtime.typeAlg', offset=1093104)

Note that the size of the DIE at offset 0x10ADCB is read as 11 by dwarfdump, but 10 by pyelftools. It's also interesting to note that dwarfdump displays more information past that what I posted, whereas pyelftools stops here.

I'm having an issue getting glances to autostart on my system. Here are my system specs and details:

Can anyone give me details on how to get this working? I'd just like to have glances auto start in the background on my system and export to influxdb.

Thanks!

The function doesn't provide favorable results on dynamically created json (values collected as json dump from mongodb)
But if the same json is printed in console and the static output is used in code it works exactly as it should.

This doesn't work
```python
response = db.reports.find({"MobileNo":No}) // mongo query returns cursor
aggregate = dumps(response) // dumps json
final['sample'] = aggregate
table = json2html.convert(json = final)
 ```

But if you copy the console output of aggregate and pass it a static string to the json2html.convert() it works perfectly
eg  `print aggregate`
> [{"eosinophils": "03", "mchc": "336", "hct": "333", "mch": "190", "_id": {"$oid": "567c385ac3ea3256121cb6f5"}, "rbc": "563"}, {"eosinophils": "03", "mchc": "336", "hct": "333", "mch": "190", "_id": {"$oid": "567c3a5ac3ea3256cb1ff2a5"}, "rbc": "563"}]

issue_title: create test data
Non-Skill Player Activity in Plays/Games
issue_body:
Hi Andrew,

Big fan of the nfl data ecosystem you built, thanks for all the work put into this.

In an effort to quantify non-skill player performance, I was searching for a way to determine the active players at the play level. I.E. running back A has a 5 yard gain on the play, the active offensive line players are [X,Y,Z,...].

I assume that play level data isn't available for this sort of scenario or else it would be found in the json somewhere, but games played could be? 

In theory, one would be able to access data from a player's game log site like -
 http://www.nfl.com/player/jamescarpenter/2495134/gamelogs?season=2011 - to pull data related to Games Played (G column). This approach would at least allow for player composition detail around an offensive line's game level performance. I.E. Team 1's offensive line performance = X, Team 1's offensive line had participation from o-line players [Q,W,E,...], thus [Q,W,E,...]'s performance = X.

I was hoping that NFLGame might have a precedent for this - any thoughts on a solution/alternatives for this task?

Thanks
-Mike

issue_title: nflgame searches by position
issue_body:
I am trying to find a way when searching for info to only include players at a certain position. issue_title: zebra stripes don't get applied if TABLE is contained in DIV with style=display:none
issue_body:
Thank you for the work on tablesorter. I have a table inside a div inside another div: ```
...
``` The inner div will initially have display:none if the outer div has the class "closed", in which case the whole table will not be visible. zebra stripes are working OK only if the table is not contained in invisible elements while tablesortifying. Ideally, the zebra classes should still be applied if the table is contained in invisible elements. I think this is caused by the way jQuery's CSS selector extension ":visible" is used in the logic that finds the rows that should get applied the zebra classes in jquery.tablesorter.js:2728 : ``` $visibleRows = $tbodies.eq( tbodyIndex ).children( 'tr:visible' ).not( c.selectorRemove ); ``` Probably not too hard to fix, my time and knowledge of jQuery and tablesorter's internals is lacking too much though. Perhaps I could trigger an "applyWidgets" event to all tablesortified tables contained in the div when the div becomes visible but I feel like that would be a dirty workaround. Not critical for me right now, after all it's just an UI thing. The other widgets I'm using here (saveSort, filter, resizable, stickyHeaders) seem to be initialized OK when the table is contained in an invisible element while tablesortifying.

issue_title: inconsistent handling of string args using YAML config
issue_body:
Unquoted strings that start with a number appear to be treated differently than when quoted, or than other strings that don't start with a numbers.  Example:

Config:
```
begin_time: 6:30
end_time: 12:59
```
Code:
```
p.add('--begin_time', type=str, default='6:30', help='filter out aggregations before start time')
p.add('--end_time', type=str, default='12:59', help='filter out aggregations after end time')
ns, args = p.parse_known_args()
logger.debug("begin_time: %s, end_time: %s" % (ns.begin_time, ns.end_time))
```
Result:
`08-08 07:08 root         DEBUG    begin_time: 390, end_time: 779`

If the values in the config file are quoted, then it returns the expected result:

Config:
```
begin_time: '6:30'
end_time: '12:59'
```

Result:
`08-08 07:09 root         DEBUG    begin_time: 6:30, end_time: 12:59`

issue_title: Same argument given multiple times in config file is not working like in command line
issue_body:
Consider :
parser.add_argument('--a', action='append')

Following args in command line :
--a 1 --a 2
Would correctly give after parsing :
Namespace(a=[1,2])

But the same args in a config file :
--a 1
--a 2
Would wrongly give after parsing :
Namespace(a=2)

In short, parsing multiple times the same arg (with action='append') from a config file is not appending values as it should (like from command line) but instead keeping only the last value.

issue_title: Fast executemany on SQL Server fails to insert more than one row at a time
issue_body:
### Issue
I am using fast executemany to bulk insert a lot of rows (>1 Mio.) containing columns with different data types into a SQL Server table. The data was already imported over pyodbc single executing INSERT Statements, but to speed things up, executemany and Fast-executemany should be used. 

You can find a sample Code including real world sample data and all DDL needed to set up the database table in the following GIST (https://gist.github.com/pinkerltm/9cb8a85a5df5c2f0ba2a6f66b8306815).

I would expect that executemany would work with 2 (or more) rows in the values list as it does with a list containing only one row. 

Instead I get the following error:

> pyodbc.DataError: ('String data, right truncation: length 89 buffer 73', '22001')
> 
> During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
> 
> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "C:/_work/Devel/streetapps-import-export/tests/scratch.py", line 82, in <module>
>     cursor.executemany(sql, params)
> SystemError: <class 'pyodbc.DataError'> returned a result with an error set

If I pass a list containing only one single row, everything works fine, although the INSERTs run much slower than simply INSERTING them one by one with `cursor.execute()`.

Also if I turn off `Cursor.fast_executemany = False`, again at the cost of execution time.

### Environment
- Python 3.5.2 (64-Bit)
- pyodbc 4.0.22 
- OS: Windows 10
- DB: SQL Server 2012

I have this code:

```python

```

And in the exit handler for the process (when someone either hits ctrl-c or run the stop process):

```python
with open(pid_file, 'r') as content_file:

os.kill(int(pid), signal.SIGTERM)

Reading the code, I see this:

```python
        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self.sigterm)

And that has this:

```python
    def sigterm(self, signum, frame):

issue_title: Use angular events

issue_title: wxPython's GetHandle() returns 0 with wxPython 3.0/4.0 on Linux
issue_body:
On Linux with wxPython 3.x / 4.x window handle is not yet available even though Show() was called and it causes in the wxpython.py example to open two windows, one blank window and another window with CEF browser. This effect is because window handle passed to CEF is 0, thus CEF creates a top-level window on its own.

The solution is to delay embedding browser by a bit and window handle becomes available then. Time of 1ms is not enough, 10ms seems fine, but to be sure I've used 20ms. Example works fine with such code:

```
        # On Linux must show before embedding browser, so that handle
        # is available (Issue #347).
        if LINUX:
            self.Show()
            # In wxPython 3.0 and wxPython 4.0 handle is still
            # not yet available, must delay embedding browser
            # (Issue #349).
            if wx.version().startswith("3.") or wx.version().startswith("4."):
                wx.CallLater(20, self.embed_browser)
            else:
                # This works fine in wxPython 2.8
                self.embed_browser()
        else:
            self.embed_browser()
            self.Show()
```

Issue originally reported on the Forum:
https://groups.google.com/d/topic/cefpython/ZBXEdOR3svw/discussion

issue_title: Using enum for clear API
issue_body:
This proposal was originally borned in our discussion [here](https://github.com/lk-geimfari/mimesis/commit/31614c6a09d964a59381fc16fc63992b976092a2#commitcomment-25635904).

Proposal (by @sobolevn):

"_Maybe we could use enums more often?
It really covers this case easily. And it is possible to statically check your code with enum._"

Here's the sample code:

```python
from enum import Enum

class PortRange(Enum):
      DEFAULT = (1, 65535)
      WELL_KNOWN = (1, 1023)
      EPHEMERAL = (49152, 65535)
      REGISTERED = (1024, 49151)
```

And then:
```python
def port(self, diapason: PortRange = PortRange.DEFAULT) -> int:
```

So, we want to discuss with community what is better? Let's discuss.

issue_title: Negative image offset leads to corrupt xlsx file when re-saved
issue_body:
https://gist.github.com/kjosib/97564bb295dd6e7f3b7a

That links to a 5-line sample program for causing this issue. Comments describe exactly how to provoke the bug. You can use this sample png if you like:
![test](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/13260087/8607322/c4c9902c-2657-11e5-8327-7c81ffc17206.png)

The program inserts a small image (208x49) with an x_offset more negative than the width of the image. (This is done to position the image relative to the right edge of the printable area, without knowing the width of all columns.) You can generate, open, and read such a file just fine. However, such a file will often corrupt if Excel then saves out modifications. It doesn't happen if the image is big enough or the offset greater than 0-imagewidth. (I'm running 0.7.3, just upgraded via PIP.)

Is this something that is even possible to address?

Another solution (for me, not for this bug) would be adding support for images in print headers.

issue_title: rewrite setup.py to `pbr` and `setup.cfg`
issue_body:
This is a proposal, not a bug.

Existing `setup.py` is already using `pbr`, probably to incorporate `test` command.

If we rewrite the `setup.py` into `pbr`, it would result in:
- all existing functionality still present
- 3-lines long `setup.py`
- all metadata moved into `setup.cfg`
- version being derived from git tag (we may decide for other method, like having version hard coded in this file or in external one)

Apart from cleaning the code, we could resolve the issue #37 by that.

issue_title: Get 'None' or a default value if key does not exist
issue_body:
Hello,

Thanks for working on this. I've just started using etcd v3 so maybe I'm missing something, but I find that the default behavior of the client's 'get' method when requesting a non-existing key is inappropriate. I think we should take advantage of a behavior similar to the Python dictionary, i.e. be able to get at least 'None' instead of catching an exception.

This is especially true when we want to test if a key already exist. Moreover, it would be more consistent with the 'etcdctl' CLI tool that does not display anything when a non-existing key is requested, an return a 0 exit code.

issue_title: Board.is_legal not recognizing legal castling moves
issue_body:
Here is a board where it is legal for White to castle kingside:

```
>>> b=Board(fen='rn1qk1nr/ppp2ppp/3bp3/3p1b2/3P4/3BPN2/PPP2PPP/RNBQK2R w KQkq - 2 5')
>>> m=Move.from_uci('e1h1')
>>> b.is_legal(m)
False
```

So the move is correctly recognized as illegal. An earlier edit of this issue hadn't noticed that the move was ending in h1 rather than g1, so I misdescribed the problem then. However, there is still an issue, for notice:

```
>>> b.san(m)
'O-O'
>>> b.push(m)
```

These shouldn't work, but they do.

I'm using python-chess version 0.15.4, but I don't see any discussion of this bug in release notes for more recent releases.

import djclick as click

@click.command()

issue_title: Designspace needs to store information about rules
issue_body:
This is a proposal for some xml to record conditional glyph substitutions in designspace.

- I'm not sure if this covers all the conditions allowed by OpenType 1.8
- this is not proposal for feature language.
- just covers single glyphs, not groups. 
- does not cover GPOS-like things. 

```
<!-- optional: list of substitution rules -->
	<rules>
		<rule name="vertical bars" enabled="true">
			<sub name="cent" byname="cent.alt"/>
			<sub name="dollar" byname="dollar.alt"/>
			<condition tag="wght" operator=">" value="250.000000"/>
			<condition tag="wdth" operator="<" value="100"/>
			<condition tag="opsz" operator=">" value="40"/>
		</rule>
    </rules>
```
## rules
`rules` element: groups all the rules. Contains one or more `rule` elements

## rule
`rule` element: a single rule. Contains one or more `sub` elements, and one or more `condition` elements. All conditions must evaluate to `true` for the rule to be
-  `name`: attribute, optional. String. Descriptive name for this rule.
- `enabled`: attribute, optional. Bool. If this rule should be evaluated or not.

## sub
`sub` element: a single glyph to glyph substitution. 
- `name` attribute, required. Name of the source glyph. 
- `byname` attribute, required. Name of the replacement glyph.

## condition
`condition` element: a single condition that has to evaluate to true for this rule to be valid.
- `tag` element: attribute, required. OpenType axis tag. 
- `operator` attribute, required. Can be either < or >
- `value` attribute, required. Number.

issue_title: Schema contraction
issue_body:
There is a useful function in the Schema class that allows extension of a schema by adding keys to it. This saves a lot code when you have multiple schema with only minor variations. Something that might also be useful is the ability to contract a schema, taking keys away. I'm not sure if this would be too specific a feature for this library, and I'm also not sure of a clean way to do it, but right now we write our own helper functions to do just this.

Is it worth pursuing?

issue_title: Add support for case backrefs in Regex format replace
issue_body: Look to add support for case backrefs in the the regex module's format options.  Also look into to adding a convenience function `expandf`.  Most likely we will try and take the route of reworking the replacement logic of `bregex` to work like `bre` and handle replacements early on.  Most likely we will take `{} {} {}` and convert it to `\g<0> \g<1> \g<2>` and also convert `{0} {test}` to `\g<0> \g<test>`.  Essentially this should work the same.

issue_title: Conditionals in templates
issue_body:
At first from the documentation, it seemed like I couldn't use conditionals in my templates (which surprised me since jinja supports these). This fails with an error from the YAML parser, for example:

```
example: {% if conditional %} It was true. {% else %} It was false. {% endif %} That's all!
```

A fix, then, is to include quotes like so:

```
example: "{% if conditional %} It was true. {% else %} It was false. {% endif %} That's all!"
```

Is there any reason one wouldn't want to surround all templates like so to avoid confusion? After making the change, I observed no change in the behavior of my application. Thanks in advance!

issue_title: Trouble with timezones/dst
issue_body:
So, I have been using celery/beat for a number of years, and have been ofsetting manually, the schedule of my tasks due to DST issues etc. As my codebase has become larger, the script that I run to change the times is getting bigger and bigger, and I have decided to sort the problem out.

So in short, my system clock updates automatically, from my shell I can run:
```bash
┌─[luke@freebsd] - [~/py3-apps/intranet] - [Thu Mar 29, 12:24]
└─[$]> date
Thu Mar 29 12:37:22 BST 2018
```

So presently I have a task to run at 10:30am, it will run at 11:30am. So I thought this would be easy, I added the following to my configuration:
```bash
CELERY_TIMEZONE = Europe/London
CELERY_ENABLE_UTC = False
```
When I run my celery beat schedule, via:
```bash
celery worker --beat -A pyramid_celery.celery_app --ini development.ini -n celeryIntranetAPI
```
Now I thought this would solve my problems, however my cron tasks are still an hour behind, how can I make celery keep up with the system clock?

Note I have tried:
```bash
CELERY_TIMEZONE = UTC          
CELERY_ENABLE_UTC = True
```
As per a few suggestions, but this did not work either.

Can anyone can shed some light on how I can link my celery cron timings to the system clock?

issue_title: v0.9.3 overrides previously added data=None feature
issue_body:
All, it seems that v0.9.3 overrides the change introduced on [20 may 2015](https://github.com/lepture/flask-oauthlib/commit/236adc092f8d2de0bf2765533ebbf4002f614039#diff-965dba1d467220edfd19a1c0424616d2) where data in non-GET requests can be none. This change causes an issue with e.g. DELETE requests where no data payload is required. 

See [client.py L#476](https://github.com/lepture/flask-oauthlib/blob/9db38f801931376b77a11db881b40bdc06abd293/flask_oauthlib/client.py#L476). 
The variable `data` is created in the preceding lines, but is not used in the subsequent request. This was fine in v0.9.2.

issue_title: Implement Multi-Objective PSO Variants
Why BSD 4-Clause?
issue_body: Trying to understand why the BSD 4-Clause is present here. It is known that the advertising clause causes [serious problems](http://www.gnu.org/licenses/bsd.en.html). So, I am trying to understand why it was added and whether it can be removed (i.e. the license converted to BSD 3-Clause or similar).

issue_title: Use metadata_lock_info data in Lock class
issue_body:
The MariaDB plugin [metadata_lock_info](https://mariadb.com/kb/en/mariadb/information-schema-metadata_lock_info-table/) allows you to query all the held locks. This could be useful as a "Who holds what locks that like..." method, e.g.:

```python
held = Lock.held_with_prefix('API.')  # A dict of lock name to connection (thread) IDs
connection_ids = held.values()
# Kill all those connection ids...
```

u = User.objects.get(...)

issue_title: 0.11.0 release
issue_body:
* [ ] Cut a branch off ``develop`` for this issue.
* [ ] Build docs locally (``tox -e localdocs``) and ensure they're current; commit any changes.
* [ ] Run ``dev/terraform.py`` in the awslimitchecker source directory to update the integration test user's IAM policy with what is actually being reported by the current code.
* [ ] Ensure that Travis tests are passing in all environments.
* [ ] Ensure that test coverage is no less than the last release (ideally, 100%).
* [ ] Build docs for the branch (locally) and ensure they look correct. Commit any changes.
* [ ] Increment the version number in awslimitchecker/version.py and add version and release date to CHANGES.rst. Ensure that there are CHANGES.rst entries for all major changes since the last release, and that any breaking changes or new required IAM permissions are explicitly mentioned.
* [ ] Run ``dev/release.py gist`` to convert the CHANGES.rst entry for the current version to Markdown and upload it as a Github Gist. View the gist and ensure that the Markdown rendered properly and all links are valid. Iterate on this until the rendered version looks correct.
* [ ] Commit all changes, mention the issue in the commit, and push to GitHub.
* [ ] Confirm that README.rst renders correctly on GitHub.
* [ ] Upload package to testpypi, confirm that README.rst renders correctly.

   * Make sure your ~/.pypirc file is correct (a repo called ``test`` for https://testpypi.python.org/pypi).
   * ``rm -Rf dist``
   * ``python setup.py register -r https://testpypi.python.org/pypi``
   * ``python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel``
   * ``twine upload -r test dist/*``
   * Check that the README renders at https://testpypi.python.org/pypi/awslimitchecker

* [ ] Create a pull request for the release to be merged into master. Upon successful Travis build, merge it.
* [ ] Continue at [#13 on the Release Checklist](http://awslimitchecker.readthedocs.io/en/develop/development.html#release-checklist).

issue_title: Is it possible to increment rate limit on one view and consume on others?
issue_body:
I'm looking to use django-ratelimit to increment a rate limit when a user POSTs to a view, and show a notice if the limit has been exceeded, on either a POST *or* a GET. Is this feature supported?

My goal would be something like so:
```python
from django.views.generic import FormView
from ratelimit.mixins import RatelimitMixin

class ExampleView(RatelimitMixin, FormView):
    ratelimit_key = 'ip'
    ratelimit_method = 'POST'
    ratelimit_rate = '3/m'

    def get_context_data(self, **kwargs):
        context = super(ExampleView, self).get_context_data(**kwargs)
        context['limited'] = self.request.limited
```

However, this raises an AttributeError when this view processes a GET, since the `limited` attribute is only applied on `request`s for a POST. I could change `ratelimit_method` to `ALL`, but I *don't* want to increment the counter for GETs.

Version 3.4.1 of progressbar2 seems to be completely broken on Windows, because it is calling `signal.SIGWINCH`, which is apparently not available on Windows.

```python
>>> import progressbar
>>> pb = progressbar.ProgressBar()

issue_title: ETA is really an ETE
issue_body:
#### Description


The ETA looks to be a countdown, not the actual time.

This might be considered pedantic.
ETE is the time remaining, ETA is the time and date an event will happen.
Arguably, ETA has been used in a countdown fashion numerous times before.
But can be really confusing with long progress items,

Reading 0x60 of 0x6000, 3.906%,  ETE: 1 day, 0:00:03,  ETA: Tuesday September 06 10:01:10 PM

e.g.  A flight from SFO to LAX takes 1 hour and 27 minutes.
The remaining flight time will count down from 01:27
If the flight leaves at Noon, it will land at arrive at 1:27pm

ETE: Estimated Time En-route 
ETA: Estimated Time of Arrival

#### Code

N/A

#### Versions

- Python version: 
3.5.1 (v3.5.1:37a07cee5969, Dec  5 2015, 21:12:44) 
[GCC 4.2.1 (Apple Inc. build 5666) (dot 3)]
- Python distribution/environment: IPython 5.x
- Operating System: OS X
- Package version: 3.10.0

issue_title: Batch size is not supported with BulkUpdateManager
issue_body: The `batch_size`, which is responsible for slicing updating data to chunks, is presented within `helper` module, but the method of the `BulkUpdateManager` doesn't accept this kwarg, hence never pass it to helper's function.

2. cme crashes

## Command string used

cme smb [host] -u [user] -p [pass] --shares

## CME verbose output (using the --verbose flag)

DEBUG Calling shares()
SMB         x.x.x.x      445    [host]           [+] Enumerated shares

SMB         x.x.x.x      445    [host]           -----           -----------     ------

issue_title: Empty user (and comments) field for revisions created outside admin
issue_body:
I'm using the `'reversion.middleware.RevisionMiddleware'` middleware setting along with `reversion.register(MyModel)` after every model definition.
All my models have a `created_by` and `updated_by` field.

For objects that are updated through the Django Admin, I get a User and Comments field that is properly filled in.
However, for objects that are created or updated through my own non-admin views, the revision is created (done by the middleware) but there is no User or Comment information. I'm especially interested in the User part.
When having a look at the revisions in the admin, a dash (`-`) is displayed in the User and Comments column for those non-admin created/modified objects.

Any idea on what the proper way is to get proper tracking who changed the object, outside the admin?

Note that the `created_by` and `modified_by` fields seem to be tracked by reversion:

	'_field_dict_cache': 
	{
	'modified_by': None, 
	'title': u'test', 
	'created': datetime.datetime(2015, 11, 11, 10, 30, 24, 682000, tzinfo=<UTC>), 
	'modified': datetime.datetime(2015, 11, 11, 11, 1, 20, 515000, tzinfo=<UTC>), 
	'created_by': 1, 
	u'id': 1, 
	}, 

Thanks!

issue_body:
Well, my problem is that everytime I try to create a revision at /admin it does not get created.

Here is my custom revision model:

```python
class VersionAccount(models.Model):
    revision = models.OneToOneField(Revision)

Here is my admin code:

```python

    def has_add_permission(self, request):

        Remove add action from admin interface.

issue_title: Best way to store the face features in a database
issue_body:
Hello, 

A few months ago, I saw the tutorial that, indirectly, originated this library, and, today, I finally had the need to implement a solution that uses facial recognition. However, I got stuck with a big problem when I started planning the data model: what's the best way to store the face features in a database (in order to optimize the search)?
Given the fact that the matches are not based on "perfect matches" (it deals with "similarity"), I'm having a real hard time to build the model. With what I have right now, I would have to load to the memory all the features' sets I have and then execute the comparisons. Given the current application that my project has, that approach wouldn't be a huge problem but... it could become one in the future.

If possible, could you guys, please, give some hints or point some articles/papers on that matter?

class Photo(models.Model):

from django.core.cache import caches

cache = caches['default']

issue_title: Reimplement based on naturally ordered sets?
issue_body:
The [new Pypy dict](http://morepypy.blogspot.be/2015/01/faster-more-memory-efficient-and-more.html) features natural ordering (by insertion order), faster iteration and reduced memory consumption.

It's based on [a design/concept by Raymond Hettinger on python-dev](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/python-dev/2012-December/123028.html) which he intended in big part [to use for sets](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/python-dev/2012-December/123207.html)

issue_title: summary_data_from_transaction_data() returns different result when monetary_value_col included
issue_body:
Hi I found that summary_data_from_transaction_data() returns a different result when monetary_value_col is included. Specifically, when there are multiple transaction (of different price) in the same day, the result will be incorrect.

I chased the code and believe the cause is:
period_transactions = reduce_events_to_period(transactions, *select_columns).reset_index(level=select_columns[1:])

which will grouped the dataframe by *select_columns, and I think monetary_value_col should not be included.

So perhaps a modification is  
period_transactions = reduce_events_to_period(transactions, [customer_id_col, datetime_col]).reset_index(level=select_columns[1:])

While at the same time, (near the end of the function):
customers['monetary_value'] = period_transactions.groupby(level=customer_id_col)[monetary_value_col].mean()

should be changed to:
customers['monetary_value'] = transactions.groupby(customer_id_col)[monetary_value_col].mean()

issue_title: is_member_of_type
issue_body:
The most useful introspection methods I'd like to use from the `typing` module are basically the functions that were removed in https://github.com/python/typing/pull/283: checking whether a concrete object is compatible with a given type. This would be useful for simple runtime type assertions, for exploring the implications of PEP484 in the REPL, and annotations-based dispatch.

Please let me know what you think; I'd be very interested in helping to implement this. I can also submit a PR if that makes discussion easier.

## Objections

### Should this be in external tools?
@1st1 [objected to this](https://github.com/python/typing/issues/136#issuecomment-104700656) on the grounds that it should be left to external tools, but it seems like implementing a reference for cases which are unambiguous according to PEP484 would be quite useful, and even helpful to building external tools of that kind. 

For cases where something _other_ than recursive `isinstance` calls are required (e.g. type variables), a helpful error can be raised at runtime.

### Mutable values
There was an objection to `__instancecheck__` and `__subclasscheck__` by BDFL-delegate @markshannon [here](https://github.com/python/typing/issues/136#issuecomment-104700131):
```
For example,
List[int] and List[str] and mutually incompatible types, yet
isinstance([], List[int]) and isinstance([], List[str))
both return true.
```

This is a bit tricky for mutable types, since `[]` is a member of `List[X]` for all `X`, but the _same list object_ might cease to be in `List[X]` when elements are added to it. For immutable types, the answer is unambiguous, so `Tuple[*]` could always deliver an unambiguous answer.

For `List[]`, `Dict[]` and other mutable types, my preference would be to check the types of the elements _right now, when the method is called_, and not at some future time when it may have been mutated. This would be a distinction between `mypy` and the runtime type checking, but this behavior seems more in keeping with the principle of least surprise, and would accord with the main intended use cases (REPL-based exploration of the type system, and simple runtime type assertions). 

## Implementation

I think the implementation would be roughly like the following:
```python
def is_member_of_type(obj, tp):
    if has_type_variables(tp):
        raise ValueError('Type variables cannot be determined at runtime')
    if is_union(tp):
        return any(is_member_of_type(obj, s) for s in tp.__args__)
    elif is_tuple(tp):
        ...
    elif ...:
        ...
    else:
        return isinstance(obj, tp)
```

## Include `issubtype` to match `issubclass`?

`issubtype` could make sense as an addition, but it would involve introducing much more of the sorts of logic a typechecker should implement, e.g. the following:
```python
>>> from typing import *
>>> class MyTuple(NamedTuple):
...     foo: int
...     bar: str
... 
>>> issubtype(MyTuple, Tuple[int, str])
True
```
For this reason, I think it would make more sense to leave `issubtype` out of `typing_inspect`.

issue_title: Namespace `decorators` are not applied to Resources?
issue_body:
I have a decorator designed to wrap arbitrary Flask view functions, and I want to apply it to every resource in an `Api` or `Namespace`.

The `decorators` kwarg in `Api.__init__` works as expected. All methods of all `Resource` objects are decorated and return correct output.

However, using the analogous kwarg in `Namespace` has no effect. I've tried every way to wire namespaces to apis to apps in the documentation, and none of them work. In all the wiring strategies, if I use the `decorators` kwarg on the outer `Api`, everything works. However, sometimes I may want to apply the decorator only to a sub-tree of the `Api`, which makes a `Namespace`-level decorator look like the obvious solution.

I dug through the source code, and I can clearly see where `Api` decorators get applied to the `Resource` methods. However, I don't see where the `Namespace` decorators factor into the picture. There appear to be no uses of the `decorators` instance attribute outside of `Namespace.init`

issue_title: Does this package support Django version 1.10?
The right way to know if the profile is expired is to look at date_joined?
issue_body:
Hello!
First I'll like to thank you guys for the support for this really helpful app!

So, my question is this:
Lets say if a user that already have a Login go to my site and he decides to change his email. So, in my point of view, it is needed to resend an activation email again, to verify if this email is valid.
So, I set the user to inactive (user.is_active=False) and call the resend_activation_mail.
The user will go to his inbox, click on the link that I sent and activate his user again.

This workflow seems coherent, right?
But is not. If you see the code for the activate_user it only active a user that is not expired. And to know if the RegistrationProfile is expired it looks based on the date_joined field, that can be months ago! So that user will no longer be able to active his profile.

An other problem that I see that can happen is, If Model User have the email field defined as unique=True and the user don't have a login yet:
The user fill his profile to create a new profile with a email that he is not the owner, so, the user will never be activate.

If another user try to create a profile with this same email it won't be able.
So a user can disable a email forever, even if the user is not the owner of the email address.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
What are your suggestions to resolve this?
Maybe could have a field in the RegistrationProfile, like date_created. And the is_expired method look for this field instead of the date_joined of the user.
I will be thankful to help!

issue_title: Unnecessary mess with class-based view prototypes
issue_body:
The [current views.py](https://github.com/macropin/django-registration/blob/62e115cf4ee21c49eb0097060839be55f48b0ba8/registration/views.py#L26) overrides many methods from Django `View` to "*pass the HTTP request nearly everywhere, to enable finer-grained processing*". Thing is, `request` is already available everywhere using `self.request`. As a result, when subclassing `RegisterView`, we have to deal with these weird prototype changes and code becomes messy.

What is the rationale for this? Would that be OK if I create a PR where I remove these (unecessary, AFAICT) overrides?

issue_title: ints shouldn't be compared with `is`
issue_body:
See e.g. https://github.com/rbarrois/python-semanticversion/blob/master/semantic_version/base.py#L94 -- it's implementation specific as to whether any given int is the same actual object as any other.

It happens to be this will work fine on CPython at the minute, which is unlikely to change its behavior, but that's not guaranteed across implementations (and there's no disadvantage to using == :).

issue_title: Consider using the offset approach used by DRF
issue_body:
In this project at the moment I have not used the `offset` approach which used by Django rest framework and inspired by David Cramer's blog post. The main reason for this is that it doesn't provide "globally unique" cursors. In particular you cannot guarantee that `before=FIRST_ELEMENT_CURSOR` will give the correct data set, as the offset of the first element cannot be calculated. This edge case could possibly be covered by a second query checking for uniqueness on that value, although this would be overkill in most situations.

From a bit of research, Disqus is the only implementation of cursor pagination "in the wild" which makes use of offsets. Both twitter and facebook use id or timestamp based approaches. By encoding the value of *all* fields used in the ordering into the cursor (rather than just the first in DRF's implementation), we don't need offsets and the resulting code is much simpler - in particular when calculating the cursor for every item on the page.

I've always tried to make sure that any paginated set has a truly unique ordering - for most applications either `id` or `created` is sufficient. It also means you can do nice things like `.order_by('-score', '-created')` where score may not be unique enough to guarantee that you don't need large offsets.

Thoughts?

issue_title: `ensure_unicode_string()` throws an exception when argument is an Exception with non-ASCII bytes in the message
issue_body:
If an object (like an Exception) is passed into ensure_unicode_string and the exception has non-ASCII bytes in the message, it will raise a UnicodeError (which will be caught) and then makes the false assumption that the argument must be a string (and therefore have a .decode() method) resulting in an AttributeError being raised.

This can happen whenever a test raises an exception that has a message containing non-ASCII bytes. When that happens, the exception gets passed to `ensure_unicode_string()` here https://github.com/box/flaky/blob/master/flaky/_flaky_plugin.py#L43 and raises an exception.

Test case:
```
def test_ensure_unicode_string_handles_nonascii_exception_message(self):
        ex = Exception(u'\u2013'.encode('utf-8'))

        string = ensure_unicode_string(ex)
        self.assertEqual(string, u'\u2013')
```

issue_title: Why is the accuracy overestimated?
issue_body:
I have trained a model, and want to see how it performs on both the training and testing data. I'm using a SoftMax output with 2 hidden nodes for a binary classification problem.

```
# predict accuracy
acc = mx.metric.Accuracy()

lenet_model.score(train_iter, acc)
print('Training:',acc,F1)

lenet_model.score(val_iter, acc)
print('Testing:',acc,F1)

output = lenet_model.predict(val_iter)
```
This yields perfect results on the training data, and not very good results on the test data

Training: EvalMetric: {'accuracy': 1.0}
Testing: EvalMetric: {'accuracy': 0.51111111111111107}

However, when I go and look at the individual predictions, I find that this accuracy on the test set is overestimated.

```
y_pred = np.round(lenet_model.predict(train_iter).asnumpy()[:,1])
y_true = phen[:ntrain]
print(metrics.confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred))

y_pred = np.round(lenet_model.predict(val_iter).asnumpy()[:,1])
y_true = phen[ntrain:]
print(metrics.confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred))
```
This yields the following confusion matrices:
[[127   0]
 [  0  76]]
[[21 14]
 [13  3]]

I can see that the training set is indeed perfectly classified. The test set, however, only gets 24 of 51 correct, for an accuracy of 47%. Why does the model.score() function tell me that I have achieved 51% accuracy?

issue_title: Query errors after initdb
issue_body:
Using a remote MySQL DB, I was able to run `airflow initdb` successfully using this connect string:

`sql_alchemy_conn = mysql+pymysql://username:password@some.thing.us-west-2.rds.amazonaws.com/schemaname` -- note use of `mysql+pymysql`.

When I try to do something basic in the web UI like click into a DAG, I get a SQL error:

`ProgrammingError: (pymysql.err.ProgrammingError) (1064, u"You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near '[(\\'dag_id\\', u\\'example_bash_operator\\')]'')' at line 1") [SQL: u'INSERT INTO log (dttm, dag_id, task_id, event, execution_date, owner, extra) VALUES (%(dttm)s, %(dag_id)s, %(task_id)s, %(event)s, %(execution_date)s, %(owner)s, %(extra)s)'] [parameters: {'task_id': None, 'extra': "[('dag_id', u'example_bash_operator')]", 'execution_date': None, 'dttm': datetime.datetime(2016, 2, 21, 11, 54, 18, 218042), 'owner': 'anonymous', 'event': 'graph', 'dag_id': u'example_bash_operator'}]`

The stack trace shows this exception being raised from the `pymysql` package. Seems like the SQL syntax is incompatible with the driver and/or the MySQL server version.

I found this problem can be resolved by switching to the `mysql+mysqldb` driver.  Depending on the client OS, this combination can be a little tricky to install, but nevertheless appears to be the solution.

Recommendation: do not allow `pymysql` during the initdb phase since it is not yet allowed in the webserver phase.  Provide advice in install doco to use the `mysql+mysqldb` driver only at this time.

issue_title: make query and subscribe work with subdirectory of watched root
issue_body:
Passing the absolute path of a subdirectory to `query` or `subscribe` (in place of a watched root) should work seamlessly.

Here's what would happen. When validating whether the path is a watched root, look at each ancestor until a watched root is found. If the path is not watched but an ancestor is, convert the given path to a `relative_root`. If a `relative_root` was also passed, prepend the converted path to the front of the existing `relative_root`.

This would let clients avoid calling `watch-list` just to find out which ancestor is the valid root. That can be done on Watchman's end.

issue_title: fetch_insights_async() incorrectly prints warnings about unsupported fields
issue_body:
This is due to the fact that FacebookRequest._accepted_fields is inferred from the target class.  See https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/master/facebookads/api.py#L581

For insights_async requests, the target class is AdReportRun, as expected.  However, in this case, the _accepted_fields should not be inferred from AdReportRun, but rather from AdInsights.

issue_title: API should show deprecation warnings to help migrate code

Plone 5 removed various tools without deprecation warnings on Plone 4; seems to me an easy way to help fix that is by adding such deprecation warnings inside plone.api.

For instance, any code asking for the [Calendar Tool](https://github.com/plone/Products.CMFPlone/issues/1320) should show a warning:

``` python
calendar_tool = api.portal.get_tool('portal_calendar')
```

This enhancement will help accelerating the adoption of plone.api and simplifying Plone internals by having a centralized place to put all of this messages.

issue_title: fixing the setup.py mess
issue_body:
It seems that the current `setup.py` is a total mess that goes against most python design guidelines. 

A good example is the fact that basic commands line `python3 setup.py --version` do have unpredictable results, as in building the package instead of returning the version, and just the version. `setup.py` was not meant to be used as fancy bash script.

This has to be fixed by using proper setuptools/distools/pip libraries not by trying to replicate some of their functionality inside the `setup.py`.

Here is a simple test case of things that are not supposed to fail:
```
python setup.py --version
python setup.py egg_info
python setup.py bdist_wheel
python setup.py bdist
python setup.py test # should run the tests, instead of the runtests
```
A full list of default commands can be found at https://pythonhosted.org/an_example_pypi_project/setuptools.html -- with the mention that the `bdist_wheel` one is added by `wheel` package itself, which I would recommend using.

Let's hope you agree with this and we will see me or someone making some pull requests that do address this.

issue_title: Unexpected stored value for multivalued fields in riak search
issue_body:
(Originally posted on the riak-users mailing list; duplicated here by request of @zeeshanlakhani in case it's an issue with the Python client)

Hi all,

I'm having an issue with stored multiValued fields in Riak search not being stored the way I would expect.

If I use the default schema, and store the following JSON document (from [the documentation](http://docs.basho.com/riak/2.1.1/dev/using/search/#Extractors))

```json
{"people_ss": ["Ryan", "Eric", "Brett"]}
```

and then search with the query "people_ss:Ryan", the document that I get back contains "people_ss": ["Ryan", "Eric", "Brett"], as I would expect.

However, if I instead create the document

```json
{"people_ss": ["Ryan"]}
```

then searching with the query "people_ss:Ryan" results in a document with "people_ss": "Ryan", where the value associated with "people_ss" is a string, not a list of strings as I would expect.

I couldn't find anything about this in any of the Github issue trackers or the mailing list. Is this a bug, or desired behaviour? Is there a way to ensure that lists with a single element are stored in Solr (and retrieved) as lists, instead of as strings?

Thanks in advance!

issue_title: Improve parser performance
issue_body:
The parser seems to take a while to compile, and another while to parse dates, especially sets of multiple dates.

The EDTF grammar is geared towards exactness rather than speed, meaning that there are strict definitions of numbers which can be used in months, etc.

There might be ways of relaxing the lexical definitions in order to improve parse speed, and then perhaps validating actual values using something simpler after parse time.

issue_title: Configurable number of processes used for parallel processing
issue_body:
When enabling parallel processing in the scheduler, a ``mutliprocessing.Pool`` instance is created by the worker [without arguments](https://github.com/spotify/luigi/blob/master/luigi/worker.py#L399). Hence, [the pool uses the maximum number of processes obtained via ``cpu_count()``](https://docs.python.org/2/library/multiprocessing.html#module-multiprocessing.pool).

In my typical workflows where I use a local machine at work, I like to have one spare processor for some interactive tasks (e.g. some shell operations, etc). When all processors run at ~100% for let's say 6 hours, interactive working is not that much fun. So I would really appreciate an (integer) option like "max_parallel_tasks" in the [core] section.

Maybe even some sign distinction could be helpful, sth like:

```python
...
count = cpu_count()
if count > max_parallel_tasks > 0:
    n_processes = max_parallel_tasks
elif -count < max_parallel_tasks < 0:
    n_processes = count + max_parallel_tasks # max_parallel_tasks is negative here
else:
    n_processes = count

pool = multiprocessing.Pool(n_processes)
...
```

(In that case I would choose ``max_parallel_tasks: -1``)

Off-topic: Nice job guys!

issue_title: Add support for streaming encryption operations
issue_body:
Currently PGPy works on payloads as a whole and not in streaming.

This causes that when encrypting/decrypting large payloads messages and files are read completely in ram making unfeasible to perform crypto operations on low memory systems or high performances systems.

This ticket is to keep track of the discussion in order to implement streaming capabilities for the library.

issue_title: Should boto3 accept `None` as meaning "Not set" for method parameters?
issue_body:
If I try to [create instances on EC2](http://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/services/ec2.html#EC2.ServiceResource.create_instances) and pass in `None` for an optional parameter like `SubnetId`, I get this:

```
botocore.exceptions.ParamValidationError: Parameter validation failed:
Invalid type for parameter SubnetId, value: None, type: <class 'NoneType'>, valid types: <class 'str'>
```

Instead, it appears boto3 allows either for the parameter to be left out from the method call, or for an empty string to be passed in.

If I am passing in parameter values to a boto3 method from the command-line via a library like Click or argparse, then unspecified options will be passed along as `None` and be rejected by boto3. This means I have to set the default value for all these optional parameters to the empty string.

That's not a big deal, but it made me wonder: Should boto3 accept `None` for a method parameter as equivalent to leaving that parameter out?

For reference, "old" boto accepts `None` in this way, though I know that's not necessarily a reason to do it this way in boto3.

issue_title: Stop using the internal git_worktree struct
issue_body:
Currently the worktree implementation in pygit2 directly re-declares and uses the internal `git_worktree` struct. 

https://github.com/libgit2/pygit2/blob/8d6940f49fbb764b3737531dd8fdf667f5faf1b2/src/worktree.h#L36-L43

It would be nice if we could avoid using this unexposed libgit2 data structure for stability reasons. 

For example, building pygit2 against the libgit2 master branch succeeds, however causes silent & dangerous incorrect behaviour due to the definition of `git_worktree` in pygit2 no longer matching `git_worktree` in libgit2 (a new field, `worktree_path`, was added to `git_worktree`).

https://github.com/libgit2/libgit2/blob/e212011b9872c52f6205d3a30b10f753c3108918/src/worktree.h#L15-L35

Unfortunately, the fields which are currently exposed as the `name`, `path`, and `git_path` properties on Worktree objects are not public at all in the libgit2 API. My current thoughts about how to handle this are the following:
1. Change the `name` property to return `git_worktree_name(self->worktree)` 
   > no change in behaviour
2. Change the `path` property to return `git_worktree_path(self->worktree)`
   > *breaking change* - `git_worktree_path` returns the 'worktree_path' rather than the current `gitlink_path`. `worktree_path` is currently ~`$(basename $gitlink_path)` - this usually results in a `.git` segment being removed from the end of the path.
3. Remove the `git_path` property, as it is not exposed in any way from libgit2.
   > *breaking change* - property removal

This would make it fall more in line with the libgit2-exposed methods, which seems desirable. Alternatively, the required APIs could be added to libgit2 to maintain behaviour between releases.

issue_title: A race condition results in get_results never returning
issue_body:
To reproduce: 
add a `sleep(2)` in `ventilator.py`
```
            item_to_ventilate = self._items_to_ventilate[self._current_item_to_ventilate]
            sleep(2)
            self._ventilate_fn(**item_to_ventilate)
            self._current_item_to_ventilate += 1
            self._ventilated_items_count += 1
```
and run `petastorm/workers_pool/tests/test_ventilator.py`. The test will fail.

A *pool mechanism can not differentiate between "we processed all items" and "no items were ever ventilated".

Not sure we need to fix due to transition to Reader v2 that uses standard Python pool executors.
tagging: @rgruener

issue_title: Coordinate format for reverse geocoding input
issue_body:
I'm always looking for symmetry on the command line, and since a JSON array is the most straightforward output of forward geocoding, it would be great to be able to consume it when reverse geocoding. For example, this (using [jq](http://stedolan.github.io/jq/)) produces JSON:

```bash
$ mapbaux geocode "Bodega Bay, CA" --access-token MY_TOKEN | \
>  jq '.features[0].center' -c
[-123.0481,38.3332]
```

And the round trip could be

```bash
$ mapbaux geocode "Bodega Bay, CA" --access-token MY_TOKEN | \
> jq '.features[0].center' -c | \
> mapbaux geocode --rev --access-token MY_TOKEN
```

There's also precedent in rio-sample for JSON formatted coordinate pairs `[x, y]` or `[long, lat]`

  https://github.com/mapbox/rasterio/blob/master/docs/cli.rst#sample

but it seems like it would be relatively easy to also accept `x y` or `x, y`. There's a pattern that could be expressed as a simple regex.

/cc @dnomadb @sbma44

issue_title: Improve configuration system to support dotenv
issue_body:
This will be a breaking change so we should take this as an opportunity to further improve current configuration system:

   * Reduce Inheritance
   * ~~Make it single-config (plus the dotenv files) and get rid of Dev/Testing/Default config setup~~
   * Update docs to add configuration examples
   * Update cli run command to support the new setup
   * Get rid of /config/app.py and use dotenv for that (just as flask does) 

We should aim to get rid of the root `/config` directory, that is environment-specific, entirely and keep that environment-specific stuff in `.env` files.

issue_title: Audits does not seem to be Thread safe
issue_body:
Hi,

I was looking for an audit log implementation and came across this project. However I do not understand why this would work in all cases. From what I understand, signals are managed and stored in memory and is the same for all threads. So if multiple requests come in at the same time you may end up with the signals from request 1 and signals from request 2 both being called for each request. 

Simply put. This is not thread safe, and disconnecting and reconnecting signals at runtime is a bad idea.

issue_title: requires keyword produces invalid METADATA when uses with 2.1 generating arguments
issue_body:
This bug has been found originally with dateutils:
https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/issues/720

This generated bad metadata as per distlib: https://bitbucket.org/pypa/distlib/issues/106/support-21-metadata

easy reproducer:
```
# setup.py content in an empty folder
from setuptools import setup

setup(name="mytest_pkg",
      long_description='hello\n====\n',
      long_description_content_type='text/x-rst',
      requires=["six"],
      packages=[],
      )
```
then use humpty which leverage distlib to parse the metadata of the wheel:

```pip wheel . && humpty mytest_pkg-0.0.0-py3-none-any.whl```

generated metadata for this setup.py is:

```
Metadata-Version: 2.1
Name: mytest-pkg
Version: 0.0.0
Summary: UNKNOWN
Home-page: UNKNOWN
Author: UNKNOWN
Author-email: UNKNOWN
License: UNKNOWN
Platform: UNKNOWN
Requires: six

hello
====
```

as per Vinay Sajip comment:

Notice the line ```Requires: six```. This does not (to his reading) conform to metadata 2.1, by my reading of PEP 566:

Fields are now specified via the Core Metadata Specification.
Added two new fields: Description-Content-Type and Provides-Extra
Note that the linked Core Metadata Specification makes no reference to a Requires field, which dates from a older metadata specification (1.1, defined in the superseded PEP 314). While distlib's interpretation - that the presence of this obsolete field disqualifies the metadata from being considered 2.1-compatible - might be considered by some as over-strict, one could also point to the Zen of Python: "In the face of ambiguity, refuse the temptation to guess."

This is related to #1374

```setup.py``` should fail when using ```requires``` in addition from arguments that generates new metadata, so that it does not generates illegal metadata.
Not sure if there are other incompatible metadata

issue_title: Difference in sys.path order, depending on how run
issue_body:
In the coverage.py test suite, I have tests that check various environmental values to see that they are they same for `coverage run myprog.py` as they are for `python myprog.py`.  One of the values is sys.path.  Recently, these tests started failing because a .egg file I installed with easy_install would appear in the middle of sys.path for coverage, but at the end for python.

I'm guessing this has something to do with #674, but I don't understand why.

The actual diff of sys.path for the two environments is:

issue_title: Is py31compat.py still necessary?
issue_body:
There still seems to be a [py31compat](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/master/setuptools/py31compat.py) file in the tree, but Python 3.1 is no longer supported. I see a few hits for it not under version guards (e.g. [here](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/69c9a3aacaefbf5f06716350fb88a226920fe51e/setuptools/command/easy_install.py#L48)).

There's also a similar one in [pkg_resources](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/master/pkg_resources/py31compat.py).

Are these still necessary?

Maybe it would be a good idea to re-organize all the `pyXYcompat` functions into a single `_compat.py` file, and put them under version guards, that way we can be confident that as soon as we drop compatibility for a version, we can drop all the compatibility shims as well.

issue_title: Provide exception on wrong metadata naming
issue_body:
I was fiddling with the python api for minio/s3 and wanted to add custom metadata to my files.
I came to the point where my files got uploaded correctly and my metadata seemed to be added as there was no error from python. When I wanted to see the stats of the uploaded files I always got empty metadata. This confused me in a really intriguing way and I spend ~3 days finding the cause of this problem until today when I stumbled over the fact that metadata on Amazon S3 needs to be named `x-amz-meta-*`.

To help other poeple tp not make the same mistake and waste time I'd like to see the python module to throw an exception when the metadata keys don't adhere to the Amazon naming convention as this is what I would expect (this clearly is an error and this needs to be pointed out by python).

If you plan to support non Amazon conform metadata please add a possibility to the module to configure an own regexp for metadata naming.

Also imho this should be pointed out in the documentation (at least with a link to http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/UsingMetadata.html#UserMetadata) for users like me that never before worked with Amazon S3 but are interested in minio itself. The implementation of the custom regexp with the Amazon naming as predefined value would also suffice as documentation.
